introSection: Bandit (Japanese: バンデット) is a steel roller coaster located at Yomiuriland in Inagi, Tokyo, Japan. Built by TOGO, it opened in March 1988 and was the world's complete-circuit tallest roller coaster and the world's fastest roller coaster (until ShockWave opened three months later at Six Flags Great America and broke the full-circuit height record and until Great American Scream Machine opened one year later at Six Flags Great Adventure and broke the speed record).

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Bandit has a very long track length, with many hills and banked curves, as well as an upward helix. It is the second longest, fastest and highest roller coaster built by TOGO, after Fujiyama at Fuji-Q Highland.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Brown track with yellow rails and brown supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The train is designed to resemble a bunch of logs.

fullText:
Bandit (Japanese: バンデット) is a steel roller coaster located at Yomiuriland in Inagi, Tokyo, Japan. Built by TOGO, it opened in March 1988 and was the world's complete-circuit tallest roller coaster and the world's fastest roller coaster (until ShockWave opened three months later at Six Flags Great America and broke the full-circuit height record and until Great American Scream Machine opened one year later at Six Flags Great Adventure and broke the speed record).

Bandit has a very long track length, with many hills and banked curves, as well as an upward helix. It is the second longest, fastest and highest roller coaster built by TOGO, after Fujiyama at Fuji-Q Highland.

Brown track with yellow rails and brown supports.

The train is designed to resemble a bunch of logs.


 * Bandit on the parks website (Japanese)
